name: Westwood
description: Walkable area adjacent to UCLA with student housing, restaurants, and the Westwood Village.

name: Santa Monica
description: Beachside city with the pier, Third Street Promenade, and a strong tech presence.

name: Venice
description: Eclectic beach neighborhood with the boardwalk, canals, and Abbot Kinney shopping.

name: Culver City
description: Studio town turned tech hub, home to Sony Pictures and Apple's West Coast offices.

name: Mar Vista
description: Quiet residential pocket between Venice and Culver City with a popular farmers market.

name: Playa Vista
description: Modern planned community on the Westside, nicknamed 'Silicon Beach'.

name: West Los Angeles
description: Dense residential area between the 405 and Westwood, mix of apartments and small businesses.

name: Brentwood
description: Upscale residential area north of Wilshire with San Vicente's shops and restaurants.

name: Beverly Hills
description: Affluent city wrapped by LA, known for Rodeo Drive and quiet residential streets.

name: West Hollywood
description: Walkable nightlife and restaurant hub between Beverly Hills and Hollywood.

name: Hollywood
description: Tourist landmarks plus a dense mix of older apartments and new mid-rises.

name: Los Feliz
description: Hillside neighborhood next to Griffith Park, popular with creatives.

name: Silver Lake
description: Hip Eastside neighborhood around the Silver Lake Reservoir with strong coffee and music scenes.

name: Echo Park
description: Lake-centered neighborhood between downtown and Silver Lake.

name: Downtown LA
description: Arts District lofts, historic core high-rises, and most of the rail network.

name: Koreatown
description: Dense and central, 24-hour food scene, two Metro lines.

name: Mid-City
description: Geographic middle of LA, mix of single-family and apartments, served by the K Line.

name: Miracle Mile
description: Wilshire corridor with LACMA and the future Purple Line stations.

name: Pasadena
description: Old Town shops, Caltech, and Gold Line access; quieter pace than central LA.

name: Downtown Long Beach
description: Waterfront downtown with the Blue Line terminus, cheaper than the Westside.
